message:
🐛 fix: recognize cookie-only sessions in feature-request demo check (#8291) (#8373)

Follow-up to #8366. The banner fix surfaced the demo-mode state
to the user, but the underlying misclassification remained:

  isDemoUser(): !token || token === 'demo-token'

reads ONLY localStorage['token']. After the #6590 / #8108
migration, a legitimate OAuth session lives entirely in the
HttpOnly kc_auth cookie with nothing JS-readable in localStorage,
so this check returned true for real users and served them the
hardcoded demo queue — which is exactly what @xonas1101 reported:

> POST /api/feedback/requests → 201, but no GET /api/feedback/queue
> follows, and the Updates tab shows the three sample items.

The POST succeeded because the HttpOnly cookie carries the JWT to
the backend; the GET never fired because the client-side demo
check saw an empty localStorage token and short-circuited to demo
data, which both skips the GET and clobbers the just-prepended
submission on refresh.

Reconcile with auth.tsx line 702, which already treats
`kc-has-session === 'true'` as authoritative for authentication.
Applying the same signal here makes real cookie-only sessions fall
through to the API path instead of being mislabeled as demo.

Fixes #8291

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,15 +1,21 @@
 import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
 import { api } from '../lib/api'
-import { STORAGE_KEY_TOKEN } from '../lib/constants'
+import { STORAGE_KEY_TOKEN, STORAGE_KEY_HAS_SESSION, DEMO_TOKEN_VALUE } from '../lib/constants'
 import { MIN_PERCEIVED_DELAY_MS } from '../lib/constants/network'
 
 /** Cache TTL: 30 seconds — polling interval for status updates */
 const CACHE_TTL_MS = 30_000
 
-// Check if user is in demo mode — no token or explicit demo-token
+// #8291 — Post-#6590, a legitimate OAuth session can live ENTIRELY in the
+// HttpOnly kc_auth cookie with nothing in localStorage['token']. The previous
+// token-only check mislabeled those users as demo and served them the
+// hardcoded sample queue. The `kc-has-session` flag is set by /auth/refresh
+// once the backend confirms a cookie-backed session, so it's the authoritative
+// signal that a real user is logged in even with an empty localStorage token.
 function isDemoUser(): boolean {
+  if (localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY_HAS_SESSION) === 'true') return false
   const token = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY_TOKEN)
-  return !token || token === 'demo-token'
+  return !token || token === DEMO_TOKEN_VALUE
 }
 
 // Types
